column6: Customer 1 - "Recently transitioned from banking to a fintech startup (~3 months), with a shift to lower base pay (~8k) plus equity and some uncertainty around income stability. Holds a large condo loan (~$1.1M, ~$4.2k/month, mainly CPF), maintains ~70k cash and ~180k in investments with regular $1.5k monthly DCA, but only has basic hospitalisation and ~300k term coverage with no CI or accident protection. Leads an active lifestyle (gym, Muay Thai) with prior minor injury, and is in a serious relationship (~1 year) with plans for marriage in 2–3 years and children thereafter."

column4: Uhh so he just changed job recently, like about 3 months ago. From banking go to fintech startup, so now his pay structure a bit different. Last time he was earning about 9.5k plus bonus maybe 3 months, now base around 8k but got equity la… so I think he also not very sure how stable long term.
column5: <Insert voice recap transcript>

column4: Then biggest thing is he just bought a condo last year, loan quite big sia… about 1.1 mil. Monthly like 4.2k, mostly using CPF. His OA now left about 60k only, SA around 110k which is actually quite okay.

Savings wise got about 70k cash, investments around 180k… he doing DCA like 1.5k every month into ETF and some tech stocks. So actually financially quite decent la, just that his protection side really quite weak.
He only got hospital plan with rider, AIA one, then term plan only 300k. No CI at all… which I think quite dangerous especially now he in startup, income not stable.
Also ah, he quite active — gym and recently do Muay Thai. He say last time got ankle injury but never claim anything because no personal accident. So that one quite obvious gap.

Then personal side… he got girlfriend, about 1 year already, quite serious. He mention maybe 2–3 years will get married, then maybe kids few years later. So actually his responsibilities will increase quite a lot soon.
So overall I think main things for him:
- confirm need to add personal accident
- CI coverage is a must
- term coverage need to increase, because now his loan already 1.1 mil but coverage only 300k, confirm not enough
He quite open lah, but also concerned about cash flow since his salary structure changed.

column5: I am an insurance advisor who is meeting an existing customer. I have some information about the customer in the database (e.g., age, income, dependents, policy holdings), but no past meeting notes captured. Generate a concise and actionable pre-meeting preparation for me for this customer, including any of the following details that are relevant: 1. Reconnect a. Previous interaction (e.g. last meeting, referral, event, how you met) b. Light personal follow-up (e.g. job, family, plans mentioned before) c. Context re-entry (e.g. “we spoke a while ago, just picking things back up”) 2. Reset context a. Time gap (e.g. weeks/months since last conversation) b. Any updates (e.g. changes in job, family, finances) c. Re-align expectations (e.g. exploratory chat, no pressure to decide) 3. Known information a. Existing products (e.g. GE plans, basic coverage if known) b. Basic profile (family status, occupation, life stage) c. Prior touchpoints (topics loosely discussed before) 4. Current situation a. Life updates (new home, marriage, career changes) b. Financial situation (e.g. income stability, commitments) c. Any additional new products d. Any new responsibilities (e.g. dependents, loans, obligations) 5. Goals & priorities a. Short-term goals b. Long-term goals c. Financial priorities d. Financial risk 6. Solution alignment a. Areas to explore (e.g. protection, savings, planning) b. Potential fit (e.g. where solutions might align) c. Interest level (e.g. early exploration vs ready to act) 7. Close & next steps a. Suggest next steps b. Set expectations on timelines etc. I want to focus on relationship building angle, less of sales push
column6: Here’s a **concise, relationship-first pre-meeting prep** you can use—focused on reconnecting, understanding, and building trust (not selling):

column3: - Does not have prior meeting notes

column6: * No notes → treat as **friendly reconnection, not continuation**
* “I realised we haven’t properly caught up before, so thought it’d be good to reconnect”
* “We probably spoke some time back, just picking things up again and getting to know your situation better”
* “Not sure when your last review was, so good chance to refresh”
* “Today is really just a catch-up to understand where you are now”
👉 Don’t present as audit—use as **conversation prompts**
* “How are things on the financial side—fairly stable?”
* “Have you reviewed or added anything since then?”
* “Anyone depending on you financially right now?”
* “Anything you’re planning for in the next couple of years?”
* “What are the bigger things you’re working towards?”
* “What’s more important to you now—growing, protecting, or flexibility?”
* “Any concerns that have been on your mind recently?”
* “Maybe later we can look at whether your current plans still match your situation”
* “Based on what you shared, might be worth reviewing ___”
* “If you’re open to it, I can help you organise everything and show you how it fits together”
* “We can take it step by step—no rush”
> “This meeting is about getting to know them again and making them feel understood—not about closing anything.”
